name: Code View
summary: Renders syntax-highlighted component source with a copy affordance — the source-code-view feature from the workbench inventory (#625), ratified by #626 as a forced block mint. The copy affordance composes the data-transfer copy intent (the copy-out half). Native-first: highlighting paints token ranges via the CSS Custom Highlight API over a tokenizer rather than wrapping every token in a <span>, with library highlighters (Prism/Shiki/highlight.js) as opt-in adapters. A live-editable sandbox embed is explicitly out of scope (that is the assembler/playground concern).

cssCustomHighlight.pattern: CSS Custom Highlight API — paint token ranges via Highlight + ::highlight() without per-token DOM
cssCustomHighlight.adoption: The native-first highlighter tokenizes the source and registers a Highlight per token class, styled with ::highlight(); no <span> soup, and the source text stays a single selectable/copyable node.

clipboard.pattern: Async Clipboard API (navigator.clipboard.writeText) behind the data-transfer copy intent
clipboard.adoption: The copy button writes the verbatim source via the data-transfer copy-out (emit) half, so the affordance is the shared intent, not a bespoke clipboard call.

nativeFirstHighlight.description: CSS Custom Highlight API over a tokenizer is the default; library highlighters are opt-in adapters
nativeFirstHighlight.rationale: Native-first default — the platform now paints ranges without DOM wrapping; Prism/Shiki/highlight.js become enhancements via an adapter, not the baseline.

copyIsTheSharedIntent.description: The copy button composes the data-transfer copy intent, not a private clipboard call
copyIsTheSharedIntent.rationale: Reuses the standardized copy-out affordance so behavior (effect, a11y, fallbacks) is consistent with every other copy surface.

noLiveEditor.description: Read-only highlighted source with copy; no editable sandbox
noLiveEditor.rationale: A live-editable embed is the assembler/playground concern (#646), deliberately out of scope to keep the catalog block focused and lock-in-free.
